message:
fix(signal): separate signal-safe delivery from async notification on Windows

CRT signal handlers on Windows run in signal context where locking is
forbidden. Previously, record_delivery() called notify_waiters()
directly inside the handler, risking deadlock.

Split into two operations:
- record_delivery_signal_safe(): atomic-only counter bump, safe for
  signal context (no locks, no allocations)
- notify_if_changed(last_seen): polls the counter and wakes async
  waiters from a safe context

A background poller thread (asupersync-signal-poll-win) runs a 1ms
poll loop checking all signal slot counters and waking waiters when
deliveries are detected.

Also import std::thread for Windows (was unix-only).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
